programmes.show_description: Danny Varrow is a great footballer but morally corrupt; he's a womaniser and a gambler, so Terry has his work cut out when his past catches up with him. S2 E11: All About Scoring, Innit
1985 Christmas special. Alf schemes so they can attend two Christmas dinners. Rita is forced to stay.
Chrissy receives flowers and chocolates from a secret admirer; it turns out that they were meant for Jo and that the admirer had wrongly assumed her to be Chrissy. S3 E5: Somebody Out there Likes Me

Hit 70s sitcom starring Jimmy Jewel and Hylda Baker as brother and sister Eli and Nellie Pledge, forced to run a family pickle factory together in Lancashire. S6, E5: The Right Spirit

Elizabeth has a new lover - wealthy businessman Julius Karekin - and the Bellamys could be about to lose Eaton Place. S2 E11: The Fruits of Love
Adrian's poems have been published and Joey receives a totally unexpected letter. Shifty tries to park a car in someone's living room. S5 E8
Harold has some big political ambitions and holds a local Labour Party meeting at home; Albert, a fervent Tory, sets out to disrupt it. S4 E6: My Old Man's a Tory
New station master Cecil Parkin is intent on making an impression at Hatley, but Jack is sure that he's already made an impression on his wife May. S1 E3: The Train Now Standing
Rigsby becomes convinced that magic is real after Philip bangs a ritual spear on the floor - while Ruth tries to offer herself to Rigsby, Philip and Alan play a trick on him. S1 E2: Black Magic
Robin's brother Norman comes to stay for two days and takes a shine to Chrissy; when the three of them go out to dinner, Robin gets food poisoning. S6 E5: Mum Always Liked You Best
Gary manages to overcome his jealousy enough to get Jeep parts for Ron, but his shopping trip is interrupted by an outbreak of racial tension. S3 E10: The Yanks Are Coming
Bella turns the front of the house into a shop using Bill's compensation, Jessie gets engaged to Arthur, and Jack marries Dolly at a registry office. S1 E7: A First Time for Everything

John Sullivan's bittersweet sitcom. Penny is slightly apprehensive about her decision to set up home with Vince again. S1 E7: Moving In and Out Again
Chrissy receives flowers and chocolates from a secret admirer; it turns out that they were meant for Jo and that the admirer had wrongly assumed her to be Chrissy. S3 E5: Somebody Out there Likes Me
Classic sitcom. Betty has to break the news to Frank that she is pregnant, and Frank goes for a job at a holiday camp with predictably disastrous results. S2 E4: Frank and Marvin
Tracey and Sharon take a trip to a spa to cope with Tracey's shattering news and try to make plans for the future, and Dorien prepares to say a long goodbye to her daughter. S11 E8: Spa Wars

Harold has some big political ambitions and holds a local Labour Party meeting at home; Albert, a fervent Tory, sets out to disrupt it. S4 E6: My Old Man's a Tory
Rigsby is looking for another lodger - one of his current lodgers, Miss Jones, is keen for her acquaintance Philip to move in because she is attracted to him. S1 E1: The New Lodger
The day of the wedding dawns - the women are racing around getting ready for the big event while Terry has his hands full looking after a nervous Bob. S1 E13: End of an Era
Arthur acquires a hot air balloon in a bid to break into the aerial advertising game, but his latest venture is hijacked. S9 E2: No Way to Treat a Daley
Tiger and Joey attempt to climb a mountain, the Maltbys decide to cut their holiday short, and romance is in the air as both Eddie and Rob bag themselves a date each. S8 E5
Paddy is back. Desperate to conceal the truth of his whereabouts, Paddy spins Mimi a lie, but he has vengeance on his mind. S6 E8
Chrissy takes Robin's place at a weekly card game while he is in hospital having his tonsils removed - and claims that a woman can play cards just as well as a man. S3 E7: Three of a Kind
There's been a spare bed in Philip's room since Alan moved out, and Rigsby has no intentions of keeping it empty. S4 E2: Fire And Brimstone
Tiger and Joey attempt to climb a mountain, the Maltbys decide to cut their holiday short, and romance is in the air as both Eddie and Rob bag themselves a date each. S8 E5
Des asks Terry to help him deliver a car; Arthur asks Terry to mind a magistrate. One of these good deeds could land Terry in court and serving time... S2 E12: Caught In the Act, Fact
Alf returns home from the funeral of Else, his long-suffering wife. Having lost her disability allowance and half the pension, life ahead is not too rosy. S2 E1)
When Mildred is away visiting her sister, George attempts to evict the tenants so that he and Jerry can convert the flat into separate units to increase rental income. S3 E6: We Shall Not Be Moved

Nellie takes Eli along to see The Doctor, only The Doctor is more interested in Nellie's behaviour, than Eli's cough. S3 E1: What Seems To Be The Trouble
Nellie and Eli agree to look after their teenage nephew Nigel, Nellie believes it's about time he learns the goings on of the birds and the bees. S3 E2: The Birds And The Bees
The Manx Electric Railway has been running for over 125 years along the east coast of the Isle of Man with the majority of Victorian and Edwardian era infrastructure and rolling stock still in use.

Arthur and Terry provide sanctuary to a young girl, Rita, and her boyfriend. S5 E7: Hypnotising Rita
Lady Marjorie can take no more as Sarah reveals that she is pregnant - has she brought a final disgrace on Eaton Place, or can Watkins play a clever game to help everybody? S2 E12: The Wages of Sin
Adrian, Billy and Shifty are in trouble with the police as Mrs Boswell has a chance meeting with retired widower Derek. S5 E9
Harold reluctantly agrees to take Albert on a nostalgic trip to France to revisit the battlefields where he fought during the First World War. S4 E7: Pilgrim's Progress
The station staff are ready to help Cecil move into the station master's house, and May has a confession to make and shares it with Ethel... but will she tell Jack? S1 E4: A Moving Story
Rigsby is dressed to kill as he takes Miss Jones out to dinner to celebrate her birthday. S1 E3: A Night Out
Chrissy and Norman have been dating for some weeks to the annoyance of Robin, who goes on a disastrous double date with Larry. S6 E6: Fire Down Below
Gary's life is on the move in both time zones as Yvonne sets her heart on a new flat, and Phoebe wants to leave The Royal Oak. S3 E11: Let's Get Away From It All
Mary has died when Jack meets old comrade Sid Hepburn, who is still in the Army; when Hepburn and a colleague take Jack to see Captain Leslie, he tries to recruit him. S1 E8: Paddy Boyle's Discharge
Arthur and Terry provide sanctuary to a young girl, Rita, and her boyfriend. S5 E7: Hypnotising Rita
Bittersweet sitcom. When Vince's lunch date with Penny ends with her pouring a pint over him, he decides to visit the Warrender home in a drunken state. S2 E1: Guilt
When Mildred is away visiting her sister, George attempts to evict the tenants so that he and Jerry can convert the flat into separate units to increase rental income. S3 E6: We Shall Not Be Moved
Frank and Betty try to get some practice at looking after children by taking Judy and Alison for a day out at the zoo, and Frank attends a maternity class without Betty. S2 E5: Father's Clinic
When Darryl dies, Tracey has to decide what kind of a send-off to give him - Sharon wants it to be nice for the boys and Dorien has got the perfect outfit. S12 E1: Knocking on Heaven's Door

Rigsby becomes convinced that magic is real after Philip bangs a ritual spear on the floor - while Ruth tries to offer herself to Rigsby, Philip and Alan play a trick on him. S1 E2: Black Magic
Bob and Thelma are on a Norwegian honeymoon far away from the influence of Terry - or so they think - and Terry is striving to get to know Thelma's sister Susan a lot better. S2 E1: Absent Friends
Arthur starts a courier service, the Daley Post, and it isn't long before its competitors start throwing the wrong sort of business at him. S9 E3: Uneasy Rider
Troy believes he is being stalked by his ex Gavin, but the threat soon turns out to be much more sinister. S8 E6
No one is sure if Shane will the same again after he's partially paralysed in an accident. Battle lines are drawn between Mimi and Kelly as they go to war over who will help with his recovery. S6 E9
Robin and Chrissy have borrowed the Ropers' car to drive to Southampton for an Arsenal match, but the car breaks down and they miss the game. S4 E1: Home & Away
Rigsby is ecstatic to learn that he has inherited his Uncle George's fortune but distressed at a clause that he must be happily married. S4 E3: Great Expectations
